Gov. Health: Senior AI & Automation Engineer/Python/AI/ML/DL/SP/D365 Developer/Snr Senior DevOps/Data Engineer

operational efficiency and informed decision-making.
 Designed and deployed scalable n8n workflows on a hybrid cloud infrastructure (AWS & GCP) to automate and optimize
processes for hundreds of UpGuard team members. kubectl scale deployment/n8n-worker --replicas=5

access and improving team productivity. n8n.run_workflow('Gong -> AI-Summary -> Notion')
 Implemented robust governance and security controls within the n8n platform to ensure enterprise-grade reliability and
compliance as the organization scaled.
export N8N_CREDENTIALS_ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(aws secretsmanager get-secret-value --secret-id n8n_prod_key)
 Spearheaded the democratization of n8n usage by creating comprehensive templates, documentation, and training
materials, enabling non-technical teams to autonomously build and deploy automation workflows.
 Architected and implemented n8n workflows to integrate with the UpGuard platform, creating reusable templates for critical
functions such as automated vendor onboarding, attack surface issue remediation, and data leak alerts.
 Architected and implemented a code-based governance layer within n8n workflows to enforce privacy and compliance,
including logic (if ($json.containsPII)) to automatically stop processing and flag workflows that handle Personally Identifiable
Information (PII) for review, proactively preventing data breaches and non-compliant activity.
 Successfully implemented and managed scalable MLOps and LLMOps solutions on AWS, driving efficiency by using
SageMaker for streamlined model operationalization and Amazon Bedrock to build and scale generative AI applications.
 AI and Machine Learning
 Develop and deploy domain-specific LLMs to enhance UpGuarder productivity, including a Vendor Risk Assessment
Co-pilot for automated questionnaire analysis, a Threat & Vulnerability Explainer for simplified risk communication, and a
Compliance & Policy Drafter for accelerated policy creation.
 Fine-tuned lightweight models on internal data from sources like Gong (customer call analysis), Notion (knowledge
management), and BigQuery (data warehousing), and deployed them via intuitive interfaces—such as internal dashboards,
Slack bots, and custom APIs—to empower teams across the organization with data-driven insights.
 Architected and implemented a code-based governance layer within n8n workflows to enforce privacy, compliance, and cost
controls, including logic (if ($json.llmCost > 500.00)) to automatically stop processing, flag workflows that exceed a daily
budget threshold, or handle Personally Identifiable Information (PII) for review.
 Leveraged PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Hugging Face Transformers to develop and deploy LLMs (BERT, GPT-4o, Jurassic-1,
Bloom) and deep learning models, including fine-tuning pre-trained models for NLP and analytics.
 Built robust predictive models and secure AI APIs using diverse machine learning algorithms (KNN, CNN, RNN, ANN, GAN,
regression, decision trees, random forests) and ML paradigms (supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement learning), applying
Scikit-learn and Keras to drive Next Best Action (NBA) recommendations and pattern discovery.
 Leveraged AWS Kiro's spec-driven development & agentic capabilities to design, build, and maintain scalable, secure AWS
infrastructure (EC2, EKS, S3, RDS, Lambda, SageMaker, Amazon Neptune) for hosting advanced Graph RAG pipelines,
vector databases, & LLMs, Using AWS Bedrock for diverse foundational models (e.g., Amazon, Anthropic, AI21 Labs).
 Integrated MongoDB Atlas as a vector database for RAG, enabling real-time and context-aware enterprise insights while
implementing security measures to prevent data loss from traditional JavaScript and new AI prompt injection attacks.
 Implemented cloud-native architectures with ECS/EKS for containerized microservices & REST APIs, ensuring efficient
model integration with enterprise platforms and streamlined data ingestion within an Amazon Redshift-powered lakehouse.

teams to independently build & deploy automation workflows with confidence — including safeguards such as automatically
flagging workflows that exceed daily budget thresholds or routing Personally Identifiable Information (PII) for review

decision-making across the firm.

detection and automated flagging to prevent data privacy breaches, ensuring compliance with strict financial regulations.

components for critical functions such as automated sentiment analysis, anomaly detection for fraud prevention, and
real-time market signal processing.

the use of new ML and DL tools, fostering a culture of data-driven decision-making and self-service analytics.

protecting sensitive financial data and intellectual property within a hybrid cloud infrastructure.

regression, decision trees, random forests, and SVM) and machine learning paradigms (supervised, unsupervised,
reinforcement learning), applying Scikit-learn and TensorFlow to drive propensity modeling and pattern discovery.

 Batch Layer: Utilize Amazon EMR (Elastic MapReduce) with its Spark capabilities for batch processing of large data
volumes into the Bronze layer of the Data Lake. This layer stores the initial, raw, and unrefined data.
 Serving Layer: Implement Amazon Redshift with its integrated Spark capabilities for near-real-time processing and analysis
on the Silver layer of the Data Lake. This layer houses refined and transformed data suitable for initial analytical workloads.
 Real-time Layer: Leverage Amazon Kinesis Data Streams and Amazon Redshift Spectrum for real-time analysis through
Amazon Kinesis Data Analytics. This layer can access both the Silver and potentially the Gold layer of the Data Lake for
advanced analytics and reporting. kinesis_client = boto3.client('kinesis', region_name='ap-southeast-2')

(MPP) with native query support and data views for real-time analysis through Azure Stream Analytics. This layer can

 Batch Layer: Utilize Amazon EMR (Elastic MapReduce) with its Spark capabilities for batch processing of large data
volumes into the Bronze layer of the Data Lake. This layer stores the initial, raw, and unrefined data.
 Serving Layer: Implement Amazon Redshift with its integrated Spark capabilities for near-real-time processing and analysis
on the Silver layer of the Data Lake. This layer houses refined and transformed data suitable for initial analytical workloads.
 Real-time Layer: Leverage Amazon Kinesis Data Streams and Amazon Redshift Spectrum for real-time analysis through
Amazon Kinesis Data Analytics. This layer can access both the Silver and potentially the Gold layer of the Data Lake for

 Designed and implemented Medallion Architecture with Data Lake in Azure:
 Batch Layer: Utilize Azure Databricks, a scalable Apache Spark-based analytics service, for batch processing of large data
volumes into the Bronze layer of the Data Lake. This layer stores the initial, raw, and unrefined data.
 Serving Layer: Implement Azure Synapse Analytics (formerly Azure Data Lake Analytics) with its integrated Spark
capabilities for near-real-time processing and analysis on the Silver layer of the Data Lake. This layer houses refined and
transformed data suitable for initial analytical workloads.
 Real-time Layer: Leverage Azure Synapse Analytics as the unified service layer, combining Massively Parallel Processing
(MPP) with native query support and data views for real-time analysis through Azure Stream Analytics. This layer can
access both the Silver and potentially the Gold layer of the Data Lake for advanced analytics and reporting.

 Serving Layer: Implement Azure Synapse Analytics (formerly Azure Data Lake Analytics) with its integrated Spark 
capabilities for near-real-time processing and analysis on the Silver layer of the Data Lake. This layer houses refined and 
transformed data suitable for initial analytical workloads. 
 Real-time Layer: Leverage Azure Synapse Analytics as the unified service layer, combining Massively Parallel Processing 
(MPP) with native query support and data views for real-time analysis through Azure Stream Analytics. This layer can 
access both the Silver and potentially the Gold layer of the Data Lake for advanced analytics and reporting. 
 Utilized KingswaySoft SSIS Integration Toolkit for MS Dynamics 365 and MS Dynamics 365 developer toolkit in VS 2017 to 
develop Dynamics CRM2016 applications, using customization & extension components to Streamlin data flows & integrate 
with SAP FinOps, S/4 HANA systems & using Liquid for templating and rendering dynamic content within web applications.

 Batch Layer: Utilize Azure Databricks, a scalable Apache Spark-based analytics service, for batch processing of large data 
volumes into the Bronze layer of the Data Lake. This layer stores the initial, raw, and unrefined data. 
 Serving Layer: Implement Azure Synapse Analytics (formerly Azure Data Lake Analytics) with its integrated Spark 
capabilities for near-real-time processing and analysis on the Silver layer of the Data Lake. This layer houses refined and 
transformed data suitable for initial analytical workloads. 
 Real-time Layer: Leverage Azure Synapse Analytics as the unified service layer, combining Massively Parallel Processing 
(MPP) with native query support and data views for real-time analysis through Azure Stream Analytics. This layer can 
access both the Silver and potentially the Gold layer of the Data Lake for advanced analytics and reporting.

(MPP) with native query support and data views for real-time analysis through Azure Stream Analytics. This layer can
